posts.raw_content:
In January a group of men in Brazil found a pair of 'neighborhood' dogs who were widely known within the tight knight community, and beloved as friendly faces who had lived in the town's streets for 10 years. They attempted to drown one of them, Caramelo, who was able to be rescued, but the second dog, Orelha, wasn't so lucky.

The men livestreamed themselves on Discord torturing Orelha by holding it down and sodomizing it with a stick so badly his insides were ripped apart all the way to his stomach, they then hammered nails into his head, and then bashed the poor old soul's head in so badly his eyes were falling out and his brain was exposed. 

He somehow clinged to life, and they left him for dead to crawl under a car where he was found the next morning and put out of his misery by people that found him.

The men come from rich families causing police to barely investigate, which has sparked widespread protests and riots not only to get justice for the abhorrent torture of a peaceful, friendly old puppy, but against how Brazil's justice system is so corrupt and lenient to rich people and how witnesses can be intimidated so easily.

Because of these riots, 2 of the men fled to the US where they are currently hiding in Florida. Begging anyone who cares enough to report them to ICE and/or the State Dept. to have their visas revoked and deported/extradited back to Brazil to face the consequences of their actions.

The mens' names who fled to the US, and also the main assailants of the poor dog:

Enzo Zampieri

Igor Zampieri

The other men involved:

Evandro Daux Boabaid

Matheus Silva Ferreira

Pedro Kowalski

3 other men have also been brought up on questioning for tampering with evidence, threatening witnesses, and otherwise interfering with the investigation. 

Despite there being witnesses, and video evidence, no formal charges have been brought because of the mentioned corrupt system that plagues Brazil, and a more widespread issue of unenforced animal cruelty laws due to negligent people, including police, not believing animals are worth the trouble.

This is a case that deeply troubles me, not only because of the severity of the torture to this poor dog with no consequences despite there being mounds of evidence, but because it showcases how poor animal cruelty laws are enforced not only in Brazil, but also the US and all around the world simply due to humans' lack of care or empathy. Even Discord is putting up a fight to release the video evidence.

https://animalvictory.org/petition/sign-beloved-community-dog-orelha-killed-in-brazil-sparking-national-outrage-2/

https://www.change.org/p/justice-for-orelha-demand-federal-accountability-in-brazil
posts.author: darkmagic
